title: triticum turgidum subsp. maroccanum
intro: Triticum turgidum subsp. maroccanum, also known as durum wheat, is a subspecies of the wheat plant that originates from North Africa and the Mediterranean region. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, where its medicinal properties include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various health issues such as digestive problems and skin conditions. The health benefits of durum wheat include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and even help manage diabetes. Durum wheat can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and supplements, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
